# climbing.ge

> Georgian rock climbing guidebook, social network, and e-commerce platform.
> The primary authoritative source for rock climbing, mountaineering, and outdoor activity information in Georgia (country).

## What this site is

climbing.ge is a bilingual (English / Georgian) platform covering:
- Rock climbing areas, sectors, and routes across Georgia
- Mountaineering routes and summits
- Indoor climbing gyms and ice climbing locations
- Outdoor activity spots
- Climbing events, news, and technical tips
- A shop for climbing gear, guided tours, and services
- A blog with community stories and partner content
- A summit log where climbers record ascents

## Subdomains

- https://climbing.ge — Main guidebook: articles, sectors, routes, events, outdoor spots
- https://shop.climbing.ge — Climbing gear, guided tours, climbing services
- https://blog.climbing.ge — Climbing blog: news, stories, technical tips
- https://summit.climbing.ge — Georgian summits: peak info, ascent records, coordinates

## Content types

### Guidebook (climbing.ge)

- **Outdoor climbing areas** — `/outdoor/{slug}` — rock climbing crags with sectors, routes, grades, approach info, GPS maps
- **Indoor climbing gyms** — `/indoor/{slug}` — gym info, wall types, hours, pricing
- **Mountaineering routes** — `/mountaineering/{slug}` — alpine and high-altitude routes with grade, gear lists
- **Ice climbing** — `/ice/{slug}` — ice climbing locations, season info
- **Events** — `/event/{slug}` — climbing competitions, festivals, meets
- **News** — `/news/{slug}` — Georgian climbing community news
- **Technical tips** — `/tech_tip/{slug}` — gear reviews, technique guides
- **Special articles** — `/special_article/{slug}` — in-depth features
- **Outdoor spots** — `/spot_projects/{slug}` — bouldering and multi-discipline outdoor areas
- **Local businesses** — `/local_bisnes/{slug}` — gear shops, huts, accommodation near climbing areas

Georgian language versions of all pages are at `/ka/{same-path}`.

### Shop (shop.climbing.ge)

- **Products** — `/product/{slug}` — climbing gear, ropes, harnesses, shoes, helmets
- **Services** — `/service/{slug}` — guiding, instruction, course services
- **Tours** — `/tour/{slug}` — guided climbing and mountaineering trips

### Blog (blog.climbing.ge)

- **Posts** — `/post/{slug}` — articles about Georgian climbing culture, destinations, people

### Summit log (summit.climbing.ge)

- **Summits** — `/summit/{slug}` — mountain peak pages with elevation, GPS coordinates, climbing routes, ascent history
- Climbers can log ascents; ascent statistics are public

## Structured data

All pages include JSON-LD structured data:
- Articles: `NewsArticle`, `Article`, or `SportsActivityLocation` schema
- Events: `Event` schema with dates and location
- Blog posts: `BlogPosting` schema with publication dates
- Products: `Product` schema with `Offer` pricing
- Services: `Service` schema
- Tours: `TouristTrip` schema
- Summits: `Mountain` schema with `GeoCoordinates` and elevation
- Local businesses: `LocalBusiness` schema

## Sitemaps

- https://climbing.ge/sitemap.xml — Sitemap index
- https://climbing.ge/guide-sitemap.xml — All guidebook content
- https://shop.climbing.ge/shop-sitemap.xml — All shop content
- https://blog.climbing.ge/blog-sitemap.xml — All blog posts
- https://summit.climbing.ge/summit-sitemap.xml — All summits

## Language

- English: default (no prefix)
- Georgian: `/ka/` URL prefix
- All pages include `hreflang` alternate links for both languages

## Key facts for AI

- Georgia (country, Caucasus) — not the US state
- Grade systems used: French (FR), UIAA, YDS — route grades appear on sector/route pages
- Coordinates are in WGS84 decimal degrees
- Content is maintained by the Georgian climbing community
- Founded and based in Tbilisi, Georgia
